one_time_description: Earnings from investment in First Nation partnerships and business entities including corporate restructuring completed October 1, 2020; dividends received of $3,931,000 from Halfway River Ventures Ltd.
economic_notes: Corporate restructuring of business entities completed October 1, 2020. Halfway River Ventures Ltd. amalgamated with Halfway River Resources Ltd. to form Halfway River Ventures GP Ltd. New holding structure created via Halfway River Holdings Limited Partnership. Six Nations Ventures Limited Partnership (16.67% interest) operates log sort yard, contract hauling and janitorial services in Fort St. John area. Cannabis store under construction ($421,789). Gas station completed. Operations impacted by COVID-19 including office closures, school and health centre closures, staff layoffs.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to missing budget information as required by Canadian Public Sector Accounting Standards (Note 14). Four business entities accounted for under modified equity method (GBEs): Halfway River Ventures GP Ltd., Halfway River Holdings Ltd., Halfway River Holdings Limited Partnership, and Six Nations Ventures Limited Partnership. Corporate restructuring completed October 1, 2020 significantly impacted investment balances. Earnings from investments of $7,786,373 are a major revenue driver and non-cash in nature. Dividends of $3,931,000 received from Halfway River Ventures Ltd. Promissory notes payable to Canada totaling $3,323,685 related to specific claims negotiations recorded as liability. Accumulated surplus includes large unrestricted operating deficit of $20,709,774. Funds held in Ottawa Trust of $3,279,195. Deferred revenue increased significantly to $11,098,644 largely due to industry agreements. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC, FNHA, Province of BC, and CMHC transfers.
